old_claim.note: Advanced Stone Tools is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: A refined dugout is a deliberately hollowed and shaped wooden hull, so basic wood shaping is the direct craft prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: McGrail describes dugouts/logboats as watercraft made by hollowing and shaping a single tree trunk, connecting the Pesse-style craft to woodworking rather than to the broad advanced-stone-tools ancestor.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the dugout as a shaped wooden hull made from one tree trunk.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old advanced_stone_tools edge skipped over the direct craft capability. Replacing it with woodworking_basic keeps stone-tool ancestry available upstream without treating the broad tool node as the immediate dependency.

ontology_before: Refined Dugout Canoes depended directly on Advanced Stone Tools, a broad ancestor that covers many non-woodworking tool traditions.
ontology_after: Refined Dugout Canoes depends directly on Basic Woodworking; Advanced Stone Tools remains upstream through the woodworking branch where appropriate.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: advanced_stone_tools is removed and replaced with woodworking_basic as the direct required craft edge.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to tool-use archaeology rather than boat construction.
A stronger source shows the represented dugouts were not shaped wooden hulls.
woodworking_basic is split so that a more specific hollow_log_shaping node should replace it.
short_reason: Basic woodworking is the direct craft dependency.
